items.title: How Often Does Child Welfare Call Police in Your State for Positive Drug Tests at Birth?
items.description: The Marshall Project found 70,000+ allegations of pregnancy drug use referred to law enforcement, often based on unreliable drug tests.

items.title: ICE Is Buying Warehouses. Communities Are Fighting Back.
items.description: The agency wants to warehouse immigration detainees. Local residents and officials are raising concerns.

items.title: Having a Baby? 5 Tips to Avoid Problems From Flawed Drug Testing at the Hospital
items.description: Hospitals routinely use unreliable drug tests at childbirth, and many parents are reported to child welfare agencies and the police. Here’s what you c...

items.title: Iowa’s Top Court Ended Court Fees in Dismissed Cases. Politicians Want Them Back
items.description: The state can no longer saddle defendants with court fees when charges are dismissed. Prosecutors are backing legislation to restore them.

items.title: The Marshall Project: Diversity, Equity and Inclusion, 2025
items.description: Our ninth annual diversity report notes developments in 2025 and lays out goals for the year ahead.
